source_jobs.title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At Lexroom http://Lexroom.ai, we’re not just building software; we’re revolutionizing the way legal professionals work. Our mission is to empower lawyers, law firms, and companies with a world-class AI product that saves time, boosts productivity, and redefines what’s possible in the legal world — and to become the new standard for legal AI across Italy, Germany, and Spain.

We’re changing the game, and we’re doing it with a team of builders who are looking for their next big challenge. The infrastructure that powers our AI is the backbone of that mission. We’re looking for a Cloud Infrastructure Engineer to make it fast, reliable, and ready to scale across Europe.




WHY LEXROOM?

 - Shape the Future: Join the team taking legal AI to the next level. Here, we don’t follow trends — we define them.

 - Grow with Builders: Work side by side with experts in AI and law in an environment that values competence, rigor, and genuine collaboration. Ego stays at the door.

 - Make a Real Impact: Your work will transform the daily lives of tens of thousands of professionals. You’ll solve complex problems that genuinely matter — and you’ll see the results.


THE CHALLENGE

As we scale across multiple European markets, our infrastructure has to keep pace without ever compromising on reliability, security, or speed. You’ll own the cloud foundation that our AI products run on: the platform our engineers build on every day and the systems thousands of legal professionals depend on. This is a hands-on role with real ownership, working closely with our engineering team to translate fast growth into stable, well-architected infrastructure.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design, build, and maintain our cloud infrastructure on Google Cloud Platform, with a focus on scalability, reliability, and cost efficiency.

 - Own our infrastructure-as-code setup (Terraform), making environments reproducible, auditable, and easy to evolve.

 - Manage and optimize our serverless architecture, from deployment workflows to autoscaling and resource tuning.

 - Build and improve CI/CD pipelines that let our engineers ship quickly and safely.

 - Strengthen observability across the stack — monitoring, logging, alerting — so we catch issues before our users do.

 - Harden our security and compliance posture (we’re GDPR and ISO 27001 aligned), embedding best practices into the platform by default.

 - Design disaster recovery and business continuity plans and implementation strategies.

 - Partner with product and engineering teams to support new features with the right infrastructure, and lead by example on operational excellence.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3-5 years of experience in Cloud Infrastructure, DevOps, SRE, Backend Engineering, or a similar role.

 - Strong hands-on experience with Google Cloud Platform (experience with another major cloud is welcome if you’re ready to go deep on GCP).

 - Solid command of infrastructure-as-code, especially Terraform.

 - Production experience with serverless and event-driven architectures.

 - Knowledge of various DB systems (e.g. SQL/noSQL, vectorial)

 - Experience designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines (e.g. GitHub Actions, Cloud Build, CircleCI, Jenkins).

 - Comfort with at least one scripting/programming language (Python, Bash, or similar) for automation.

 - A working understanding of cloud networking, security, and cost management.

 - A pragmatic, ownership-driven mindset: you measure your work by outcomes, not effort, and you tell the truth even when it’s uncomfortable.

 - Fluency in Italian and English.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with AI-based coding (e.g. Cursor, or Claude Code)

 - Experience with AI and LLMs.

 

WHAT SETS US APART

Our focused teams operate with purpose and autonomy, so every contribution delivers visible results.

You’ll collaborate with people who pair innovation with rigor, cur

Today, Lexroom is a trusted partner, shaping the future of legal AI with discipline and purpose.




WHY LEXROOM http://Lexroom.ai?

 - Deliver Meaningful Results: Your work will directly influence how legal professionals operate—faster, clearer, and more efficiently.

At Lexroom http://Lexroom.ai, we leave buzzwords behind. Here, you’ll build something that lasts: a trusted platform, a strong reputation, a new standard for the legal world.




YOUR KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

As a Customer Success Manager, you'll act as an Account Manager with a focus on:

 - Becoming an expert on Lexroom http://Lexroom.aiand a product specialist, guiding our customers from the onboarding stage to the renewal, including upsells.

 - Managing the entire funnel for existing business. Your mission will be to manage customers, conduct checkpoints/interviews and do upsells.

 - Working closely with customers to get their feedback, ensuring that their needs are met and that they are satisfied with the company’s product: you will be the main point of contact between the company and our customers.

 - Preparing dashboards, monitoring KPIs, collaborating with internal teams (e.g. product development, marketing,..), sharing useful insights and ideas on defining our processes




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - You have 0-1 year of experience in a Sales, Ops, or customer-facing roles

 - You are familiar with sales support tools (HubSpot, Notion, etc.)

 - You never stop learning and challenging yourself, and you’re eager to join a rapidly growing startup

 - Optional but a bonus: You have a law degree.

 - You speak both Italian and English.




WHAT SETS US APART

An Exceptional Team

At Lexroom http://Lexroom.ai, you’ll work alongside top talent in tech and legal AI.

Our focused teams operate with purpose and autonomy, so every contribution delivers visible results.

You’ll collaborate with people who pair innovation with rigor, curiosity with discipline, ambition with depth.



Growth that Matters

We hire exceptional people to make a difference—and we give them space to lead.

Your work will shape the future of legal technology and set new standards for the field.

Impact is defined by vision and execution, not by age or title. If you’re ready to take responsibility, you’ll find real opportunities to grow and lead high-stakes projects.



Rewarding Your Contribution

We value excellence and share success:

 - Annual Salary Range: €30,000 - €40,000 OTE

 - Equity Package: Align your growth with the company’s long-term success.




BENEFITS THAT MATTER

 - Hybrid Work: Flexibility to balance remote work with in-person collaboration at our Milan HQ.

 - Health Insurance: Comprehensive coverage to ensure peace of mind.

 - Meal Vouchers: Daily support to make your lunch breaks easier.

 - Performance Bonuses: Rewards tied directly to measurable results.

 - Team Retreats & Off-sites: Dedicated time to connect, collaborate, and spark bold ideas.



At Lexroom http://Lexroom.ai, ambition meets discipline and purpose. We’re not just building a product—we’re setting a new standard for the legal world. If you’re ready to contribute to something lasting and meaningful, we’d love to hear from you.

 - Strong coaching craft: you can diagnose why performance is low (skill vs will vs territory vs messaging) and fix it with a plan.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
At Lexroom, http://Lexroom.ai we set the bar for legal AI—clear, fast, and built for trust.

In just a few years, what began as an idea has become a reliable platform adopted by thousands of legal professionals across Europe.

Our mission is clear: make the law faster, clearer, and more reliable, giving lawyers, law firms, and companies the time and confidence to focus on what truly matters.

We're scaling rapidly, backed by top-tier investors and a world-class team dedicated to rigor, clarity, and impact.

Today, Lexroom is a trusted partner, shaping the future of legal AI with discipline and purpose.

 


WHY LEXROOM http://Lexroom.ai?

 - Shape the Standard: Be part of the team setting the benchmark for legal AI in Europe.

 - Work with the Best: Collaborate with exceptional colleagues in a culture that values competence, curiosity, and ambition.

 - Deliver Meaningful Results: Your work will directly influence how legal professionals operate—faster, clearer, and more efficiently.

At Lexroom http://Lexroom.ai, we leave buzzwords behind. Here, you'll build something that lasts: a trusted platform, a strong reputation, a new standard for the legal world.




ROLE

You'll own the product for two distinct but connected areas: the AI Legal product surface (legal research, AMA, Word Add-in, Regulatory Alerts) and one of our international expansion markets (Germany or Spain).
That means doing the discovery work to understand what Italian law firms actually need, shipping features that move retention and depth of use, and helping figure out what "Lexroom in Germany" means, from corpus coverage to onboarding to positioning. You'll report to the Head of Product and work directly with engineering, design, and legal engineers embedded in our squad model.



WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Run discovery: user interviews, behavioral data, support signal — enough to know what's worth building before you commit the team.

 - Define and maintain the roadmap for your area. Write specs that engineering can build from without a follow-up meeting.

 - Drive international expansion work: competitor analysis, market fit assessment, localization gaps, go-to-market coordination with sales and marketing.

 - Own launch quality: brief stakeholders, define success metrics, close the feedback loop after shipping.

 - Track what matters. Not vanity metrics — activation, retention, feature depth.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2–4 years as a Product Manager, ideally in B2B SaaS.

 - You've shipped features end-to-end and can point to the outcome, not just the output.

 - Comfortable working from ambiguous signals: you can turn a messy interview or a confusing data cut into a clear hypothesis.

 - Fluent in Italian and English — both markets, both stakeholders.

 - Structured thinker. You write clearly, prioritize explicitly, and push back when something doesn't add up.

 - Familiarity with product tooling (Linear, Figma, Notion, any analytics platform) is fine; we care more about judgment than tool fluency.

 - AI-native PM: you are able to build a working MVP using AI coding tools

Bonus if you have: experience with legal tech, enterprise software, or a previous international market expansion.




WHAT TO EXPECT

 - Scope: You'll own a product area from day one, not assist on someone else's roadmap.

 - Team: Small squads, fast feedback, direct access to the founders and CTO.
   
   


REWARDING YOUR CONTRIBUTION

We value excellence and share success:

 - Annual Salary Range: €45,000 – €80,000+ depending on seniority

 - Stock option package: Your hard work will directly contribute to—and benefit from—our success.
   


BENEFITS THAT MATTER

 - Hybrid Work: Flexibility to balance remote work with in-person collaboration at our Milan HQ.

 - Health Insurance: Comprehensive coverage to ensure peace of mind.

 - Meal Vouchers: Daily support to make your lunch breaks easier.

 - Performance Bonuses: Rewards tied directly to measurable results.

 - Team Retreats & Off-s

Our mission?

To empower lawyers, law firms and companies with a world-class AI product that saves time, boosts productivity, and redefine what’s possible in the legal world.

To become the most innovative AI company in Italy.

To build the new sales standard, the sales school for B2B sales in Italy.

We’re changing the game, and we’re doing it with a team of rockstars who are looking for their next big challenge and ambition.

 


WHY LEXROOM http://lexroom.ai/?

 - Shape the Future: Be part of the team that’s leading the AI revolution for legal professionals.

 - Grow with Rockstars: Collaborate with industry-leading talents in an environment that values creativity, ambition, and growth.

 - Make an Impact: Your work will directly influence how lawyers around the world practice law—more efficiently, effectively, and intelligently.

At Lexroom http://lexroom.ai/, we don’t just promise career growth; we promise a journey of learning, impact, and purpose. Ready to change the world of law?




THE ROLE

You are the single point of accountability for internal IT and the workplace as we scale from 200 to 300+ people across 4+ countries. You own device lifecycle, access, company software, and the technical experience of all-hands and internal events. You build the foundations (IdP, MDM, inventory, processes) and make them scale with growth.

We are looking for a builder: someone who has already set up or structured IT ops in a growing company, not only operated a mature stack.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

Foundations (first months)

 - Select and set up an IdP (e.g. Google Workspace, Okta) and MDM for device management

 - Build a complete asset inventory and its lifecycle

 - Put onboarding/offboarding on a process, with tracked and timely access removal (security-relevant)

 - Define the support model that fits Milan and the out-of-office teams best as we grow

IT Operations & Support

 - Point of reference for day-to-day technical support (hardware, software, connectivity) in Milan

 - Remote support and coordination of local hands for Barcelona, Madrid and Berlin

 - SLAs and a reusable internal knowledge base

Onboarding / Offboarding & access

 - Devices and access ready for every new hire's day one, across all countries

 - Tracked account provisioning and deprovisioning

 - Asset inventory and lifecycle (purchase, assignment, reassignment, decommissioning)

SaaS & Vendor Management

 - Ownership of company-level software subscriptions: licenses, renewals, costs, seats

 - Manage IT vendors (hardware and software procurement), tracking performance and spend

 - Multi-country hardware logistics: shipping, customs, VAT, local warranties

Workplace & AV

 - Set up and run audio/video equipment for all-hands and company-wide presentations

 - Meeting rooms: booking system, microphones, screens, room reliability


MUST-HAVE

 - Experience in IT ops / IT support at a growing company, with at least one setup built or structured independently

 - Comfort with MDM and an IdP (Google Workspace, Okta or similar)

 - Vendor and budget management

 - Comfort with on-site work on AV and events

 - Fluent Italian and English


NICE-TO-HAVE

 - Experience in multi-country (EU) environments

 - Exposure to security/compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC2)

 - Spanish or German


OUT OF SCOPE

 - Security architecture and compliance policy definition (owned by Eng/Security; you own operational IAM)

 - Internal software development


SUCCESS IN THE FIRST MONTHS

 - 90 days: onboarding/offboarding on a process and tracked; complete asset inventory; IdP and MDM live; support model defined

 - 6 months: SaaS map and spend under control with a rationalization plan; device-ready onboarding across all countries within SLA; reliable all-hands and meeting rooms


WHAT SETS US APART

An Exceptional Team
